description_long: This object is a military-style, shoulder-fired anti-tank weapon, commonly known as a bazooka or rocket launcher. It features a long, cylindrical green metal tube with a slightly weathered, camouflaged finish and visible red markings along its length. The tube is open at both ends, with a wire-frame blast shield at the front and a simple rear sight at the back. There are two prominent handles: a forward grip and a rear trigger grip, both attached to the underside of the tube, along with a rectangular firing mechanism housing. The overall construction is robust, with utilitarian design elements for durability and ease of use in urban combat scenarios. The surface appears hard and matte, with some minor scuffing and wear indicative of field use.
description: A green, cylindrical, shoulder-fired anti-tank weapon with wire-frame blast shield, dual grips, and a rugged, weathered finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the launcher, both grips, the firing mechanism, and the wire-frame blast shield at the front.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the wire-frame blast shield, with minimal detail of the body visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tube, grips, and firing mechanism, with the rear sight and end of the barrel visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the open end of the barrel and the alignment of the tube, with the wire-frame blast shield visible at the far end.
